t_ch: 那就是外卖出了问题？
t_en: So, something is wrong with the takeout?

t_ch: 你说得没错，问题的确就出在咱家的外卖配送一事上……
t_en: Exactly, something is terribly wrong with the delivery...

t_ch: 可能送得慢，菜都凉了…
t_en: Maybe the delivery was so slow that the food was all cold?

t_ch: 这怎么可能呢，我都是仔细打包好放在保温食盒里的，再说机巧鸟送货的速度可不慢。
t_en: But that's impossible. I packed all my dishes carefully and put them in thermal lunch boxes. And as you know, cycranes fly fast.

t_ch: 我把所有投诉都看了一遍，我发现投诉的内容不是说「外卖丢了」就是「送错餐品」。
t_en: Actually, I read through all the complaints, and all of them were about "lost delivery" or "wrong dishes delivered."

t_ch: 我估摸着应该是在外卖配送环节上出了问题。物流公司那边派了这只谛听过来，让我顺着订单的路线去查…但我眼下手里还有一堆订单要处理，实在是抽不开身了。
t_en: I realized something was wrong with the delivery, so I contacted the delivery company. They sent me this Diting and asked me to follow the route and find out what had happened... But now I'm having too many orders to look into this issue.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你能帮我查一下吗？拜托了……
t_en: Could you please look into this for me, {NICKNAME}? I could really use your help...

t_ch: 食盒孤零零地被丢在此处，它的外壳和等不到外卖的顾客的心一样冷。
t_en: A lunch box is left here alone. Its casing is as cold as the hearts of the customers who haven't received the food they ordered.

t_ch: （捡起外卖食盒）
t_en: (Pick up the lunch box.)

t_ch: 你仔细检查了一遍，确信这就是「尚滋味」遗失的外卖。
t_en: After checking it carefully, you are certain that it is the missing takeout food from the Spices Supreme.

t_ch: （虽然没找到「嫌疑人」，但还是先跟燕翠说一声吧……）
t_en: (Although I haven't figured out who did this yet, I should inform Yancui anyway...)

t_ch: 尽管这里没有摆放任何「此路不通」的标语牌，但堆积的杂物显而易见地提醒着你。
t_en: While there is not a "No Entry" sign, the piles of clutter here have clearly expressed this.

t_ch: （谛听闻到的气息和墙根相连，偷外卖的家伙该不会是飞过去吧？）
t_en: (The smell Diting detected is from the other side of the wall. Did the guy who stole the takeout food fly over this clutter?)

t_ch: （可我又没长翅膀，算了，还是从旁边绕过去看看吧。）
t_en: (But I don't have wings... Well, I guess I'll have to take a detour.)

t_ch: 咱辛辛苦苦做的菜啊……
t_en: Agh... I spent so much time on this...

t_ch: 您好，{NICKNAME}大人，我在这里等候您多时了。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. I've been waiting for you here for quite a while.

t_ch: 你们十王司这么神出鬼没的吗？
t_en: Is the Ten-Lords Commission always so mysterious?

t_ch: 只是{NICKNAME}大人没注意罢了。
t_en: It's {NICKNAME} who didn't pay enough attention.

t_ch: …你是什么时候出现在身后的？
t_en: ...When did you appear behind me?

t_ch: 只是{NICKNAME}大人没注意罢了。
t_en: It's {NICKNAME} who didn't pay enough attention.

t_ch: 你好守灵，这么巧啊你也遛弯？
t_en: Hello Shouling. What a coincidence. Out for a walk too?

t_ch: 守灵不是出来遛弯的。
t_en: Shouling isn't out for a walk.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人上次使用「降魔机巧鸟」成功回收天上的「逸散岁阳」，这次守灵也要拜托大人将绥园各处的逸散的岁阳收集完整。
t_en: You successfully retrieved the dispersed heliobi in the sky using the exorcist cycranes last time, {NICKNAME}. This time, Shouling would like to ask you to again collect all the dispersed heliobi around Fyxestroll Garden.

t_ch: 你们十王司挺会差遣人…
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission sure loves to delegate chores...

t_ch: 您放心，奖励少不了您的。
t_en: Don't worry. You shall get enough compensation.

t_ch: 这园子里到处都有小岁阳…
t_en: There are baby heliobi all over this garden...

t_ch: 放心，那些岁阳都在十王司看管之中。
t_en: Don't worry. Those heliobi are under the control of the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 这次拜托大人回收的是那些<color=#dbc291ff>异色的「逸散岁阳」</color>，绝非是随处可见的岁阳。大人操作「降魔机巧鸟」后一眼便知。
t_en: What we would like to ask you to retrieve this time are the <color=#dbc291ff>dispersed heliobi of different colors</color> this time. These aren't the heliobi you can see anywhere. You will instantly understand when you start to control the exorcist cycranes.

t_ch: 等到大人完成到一定程度后，守灵会带着谢礼出现——守灵会在需要时出现，不需要时离开，不会耽误大人时间的。
t_en: When you have collected enough, Shouling will appear with a token of appreciation. Shouling will appear when there is a need, and leave when there isn't, without taking up too much of your time.

t_ch: 您好，{NICKNAME}大人，我在这里等候您多时了。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. I've been waiting for you here for quite a while.

t_ch: 感谢您的协助，十王司成功回收此处的「逸散岁阳」，谢礼就留在此地，希望大人笑纳。
t_en: Thank you for your assistance. The Ten-Lords Commission has successfully retrieved the dispersed heliobi at this area. I shall leave your reward here. I hope you receive it well.

t_ch: 守灵有事便先行一步了。
t_en: Shouling has other matters to attend to and shall take leave for now.

t_ch: 您好，{NICKNAME}大人，我在这里等候您多时了。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. I've been waiting for you here for quite a while.

t_ch: 你总是这样，有点吓人。
t_en: You keep doing that. It's rather scary.

t_ch: 大人只是太过专注，没有注意到守灵罢了。
t_en: You were merely too deep in thought to notice Shouling's presence.

t_ch: 感谢您的协助，十王司成功回收此处的「逸散岁阳」，谢礼就留在此地，希望大人笑纳。
t_en: Thank you for your assistance. The Ten-Lords Commission has successfully retrieved the dispersed heliobi at this area. I shall leave your reward here. I hope you receive it well.

t_ch: 守灵有事便先行一步了。
t_en: Shouling has other matters to attend to and shall take leave for now.

t_ch: 您好，{NICKNAME}大人，我在这里等候您多时了。
t_en: Hello, {NICKNAME}. I've been waiting for you here for quite a while.

t_ch: 这些岁阳找起来可麻烦了…
t_en: These heliobi sure are troublesome to find...

t_ch: 是这样的吗？{NICKNAME}大人真是帮了大忙了。
t_en: Is that so? {NICKNAME} has been such a great help.

t_ch: 这么简单的事情你们也能做吧？
t_en: Can't you even do something as simple as this?

t_ch: …这并非简单之事，{NICKNAME}大人过谦了。
t_en: ...It is far from simple. {NICKNAME} is being modest.

t_ch: 感谢您的协助，十王司成功回收此处的「逸散岁阳」，谢礼就留在此地，希望大人笑纳。
t_en: Thank you for your assistance. The Ten-Lords Commission has successfully retrieved the dispersed heliobi at this area. I shall leave your reward here. I hope you receive it well.

t_ch: 守灵有事便先行一步了。
t_en: Shouling has other matters to attend to and shall take leave for now.

t_ch: 搏击俱乐部挂着不少沙袋，一是用来烘托气氛，二是供选手们赛前热身。
t_en: The Fight Club has lots of punchbags hanging around, both to create a cool vibe and to help fighters warm up.

t_ch: 你看着这个沙袋，忽然涌现出了一个念头——是否可以在这里尝试一下那本《贝洛伯格古武术残卷》上记载的秘籍？
t_en: Looking at this punchbag, a lightbulb goes off in your head: Could you use this to try out some of the secret techniques shared in the "Fragmented Record of Belobog Ancient Martial Arts"?

t_ch: 毕竟鬼知道这前言不搭后语的残卷上记载的「古武术」可不可靠，搞不好就是史考特那个老东西自己编造的。
t_en: After all, who knows if the "ancient martial arts" recorded on these disjointed and fragmented scraps are reliable or not. It could all be a fabrication of that old joker, Scott.

t_ch: 若是在实战中尝试，未免太危险了。不如，先在这里试试看吧。
t_en: Trying it out in combat would be risky. Better to first put it into practice here.

t_ch: 你又站在了这个沙袋的面前…大概是想再试试那本《贝洛伯格古武术残卷》上记载的招式吧？
t_en: Standing before the punchbag again... Probably wanting to try out those moves again from the "Fragmented Record of Belobog Ancient Martial Arts," right?

t_ch: （两记上勾拳）
t_en: (Two uppercuts)

t_ch: 你以最快的速度，狠狠打出了两记上勾拳。
t_en: You throw out two brutal uppercuts as fast as you can.

t_ch: （两记下勾拳）
t_en: (Two hooks)

t_ch: 你气沉丹田，打出了两记下勾拳，拳风锐利如刀。
t_en: You muster your strength and throw two hooks into the punchbag. The force cuts through the air like a knife.

t_ch: （先左勾拳，再右勾拳）
t_en: (Left hook, then right)

t_ch: 你打出一记左勾拳，紧接着又是一记右勾拳。
t_en: You throw out a left hook, closely followed by a right hook.

t_ch: （先右勾拳，再左勾拳）
t_en: (Right hook, then left)

t_ch: 你打出一记右勾拳，紧接着又是一记左勾拳。
t_en: You throw out a right hook, closely followed by a left hook.

t_ch: 几招下去，你竟恍惚间觉得自己并非在这窄小的搏击俱乐部中与沙袋较劲，而是在广阔的战场上与敌人死斗。
t_en: After several moves, you start to vaguely feel as though you weren't merely boxing a punchbag in the Fight Club, but rather were fighting an enemy to the death on a vast battlefield.

t_ch: 看起来…这「残卷」并没有发挥出它应有的作用。感觉被史考特这老东西给骗了。
t_en: Looks like... this "fragmented record" has not served its intended purpose. Feels like a trick from that old clown Scott.

t_ch: 不过，也可能是你没有严格按照残卷上的记载来训练？也许可以下次再来试试看。
t_en: But then again, perhaps you haven't been following the training manual strictly enough? Perhaps you can try again one more time.

t_ch: 又是这熟悉的搏击俱乐部，又是这熟悉的沙袋…不如在这里用那神秘的古代「斗技」来热热身吧。
t_en: The same old Fight Club once again, with its familiar punchbag... Why don't we warm up using those mysterious and ancient techniques of "martialism"?

t_ch: （开始想象训练）
t_en: (Start envisioning your training)

t_ch: 还有别的事。
t_en: Nah, I've got better stuff to do.

t_ch: 真是一场酣畅淋漓的战斗！
t_en: What a sumptuous fight that was!

t_ch: 看起来这「残卷」竟然是货真价实的武功秘籍。不过这个招式的作用似乎与想象中不同——并非用来在实战中战胜敌人，而是用来在训练中模拟实战。
t_en: Looks like this "fragmented record" really is a genuine lost martial arts manual. But these moves seem to be for a different purpose than you expected: Not useful in actual combat, but rather used to simulate actual combat training.

t_ch: 真是个好东西啊，看起来以后也可以用这招来进行战斗训练了。史考特真是难得办了件人事。
t_en: What a fantastic treasure. Looks like we can use these moves to train our combat skills in the future. Scott really has put together something rare here.

t_ch: 搏击俱乐部全新赛季现已开始！若您想要了解详情，或是参加比赛，都请随时告诉我！掘掘博士竭诚为您服务！
t_en: An all-new season of the Fight Club has begun! If you want more information or to join the matches, feel free to ask me about it! I will do my best to provide you with all you need!

t_ch: 能给我讲讲比赛规则吗？
t_en: Can you please tell me about the tournament's rules?

t_ch: 当然没问题，您想问什么问题呢？
t_en: Of course. What would you like to know more about?

t_ch: 积分赛的具体规则是什么？
t_en: What are the rules for the point-based tournament?

t_ch: 您听说过「天梯排行榜」吗？也就是记录了每位拳手比赛积分的榜单。您的积分越高，在榜上排的位置也就越高。只要您在对战中取胜，就能获得一定积分。
t_en: Have you heard of the Ladder Leaderboard? It's a list recording every boxer's match points. The higher your points, the higher the rank. You will earn points for every battle you win.

t_ch: 换句话说，分数越高的拳手实力越强，您和对手皆是如此。这也意味着随着您胜利的次数增加，战斗强度也会随之增大——不过，这点我猜您早有准备，对吧？
t_en: In other words, the higher the points, the stronger the boxer, for both you and your opponent. It also means that the more you win, the harder it gets. But I guess you're ready for that, right?

t_ch: 此外，我们还为不同实力的选手分别设置了不同的赛事，难度由低到高依次为——
t_en: Side note, we've also prepared different leagues for participants of different strengths, and they are, in order...

t_ch: 「羽量级」、「轻量级」、「沉量级」、「中量级」、「重量级」——每个排行榜的积分各自独立计算！
t_en: Featherweight, lightweight, welterweight, middleweight, and heavyweight... And the score on each board is calculated individually!

t_ch: 您可以任意选择其一，与不同水平的选手对垒。只要您愿意，更可以将自己在全部赛事中的排名全部提升至第一，从而将赛季全部奖励收入囊中！
t_en: You can choose one of them and face off with opponents of different levels. You can even place first in all of the matches and take all the rewards of the season by yourself!

t_ch: 不过，五大赛事将依次隔天开启——若您想要体验更加激烈的战斗，可千万别扑空了！
t_en: However, each of the five leagues will begin during the subsequent days on an alternating schedule. If you want to experience a heart-racing battle, don't miss out!

t_ch: 「斗技表演赛」又是什么？
t_en: What's the "Martial Exhibition" all about?

t_ch: 「斗技」是个贝洛伯格古武术的术语。是一种不依靠蛮力，注重技巧的综合格斗技术。
t_en: "Martialism" is a term from classical Belobogian martial arts. It's a mixed martial arts form that focuses on technical skill rather than brute force.

t_ch: 老板从古籍文献中找到了一本关于古代斗技的书，于是萌生了开办「斗技表演赛」，推广旧文化的念头。
t_en: The boss found this ancient manual of ancient training techniques, so he came up with this idea of a Martial Exhibition to promote ancient culture.

t_ch: 我不觉得他有这么高尚…
t_en: I don't think he was being that noble...

t_ch: 怎么听着像编的故事？
t_en: Why does that sound completely made up?

t_ch: 哎呀，真的假的不重要。反正冠军奖金是真的。
t_en: *sigh* Who cares if the story's real or not. I'll tell you what, the first prize sure is real.

t_ch: 总之呢，古代斗技分为「固、烈、流、震、刚、心、气」七种流派。
t_en: Anyway, the ancients split their Martialism up into seven different schools: "Solidity, Heat, Flow, Thunder, Firmness, Heart, and Gust."

t_ch: 每场表演赛都对应一种流派，您可以任意选择其一进行挑战。
t_en: Each exhibition match is associated with a particular school of martialism. You can freely choose one to challenge.

t_ch: 顺利赢得全部「斗技表演赛」的选手，不仅可以获得奖金，还可以获得史考特商业联盟提供的神秘奖品！
t_en: Any contestant who beats all the Martial Exhibition matches not only wins the tournament prize, but there's also a special secret prize from the Scott Business Alliance!

t_ch: 不要听我在这里干巴巴地解释了！您还是亲自去擂台上体验一下「斗技」的魅力吧！
t_en: But don't just listen to all these dull details from me! Get in the ring and experience the charm of "martialism" for yourself!

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all questions I have.

t_ch: 我想参加比赛！
t_en: I want to join!

t_ch: 没问题，我立刻为您安排！
t_en: No problem. I'll make arrangements immediately!

t_ch: 我没什么想要了解的。
t_en: There's nothing I would like to ask about.

t_ch: 若您有想法了，随时与我联系即可！
t_en: No worries. If a question does arise, feel free to ask me anytime!

t_ch: 真是太精彩了！让我们把欢呼声献给，将贝洛伯格古代「斗技」融会贯通的综合格斗大师——
t_en: That's amazing! Lay-dees and gentlemen, let's give it up for the master of mixed martial arts, the champion of ancient Belobogian martialism —

t_ch: <size=36>{NICKNAME}！</size>
t_en: <size=36>{NICKNAME}!</size>

t_ch: <size=36>{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！{NICKNAME}！</size>
t_en: <size=36>{NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}! {NICKNAME}!</size>

t_ch: 恭喜您，{NICKNAME}！您是本届「斗技表演赛」当之无愧的冠军！请收下这份丰厚的奖励，以及——史考特商业联盟提供的神秘奖品！
t_en: Congratulations, {NICKNAME}! You are the undisputed champion of the inaugural Martial Exhibition! Please accept your bounteous reward, and also — here's a secret prize for you from the Scott Business Alliance!

t_ch: 记住——史考特商业联盟，只为最强的你！
t_en: Remember, the Scott Business Alliance is only made for you, the strongest!

t_ch: 听说你有事找我…
t_en: I heard you are looking for me...

t_ch: 我听其他人说「有困难找{NICKNAME}」，就想着不抱期望地试试看…报酬什么的都好说，就是不知道你能不能搞得定。
t_en: Yep. I've heard others say "When in trouble, look for {NICKNAME}," so I thought I'd give it a try even though I didn't know what to expect... Of course, you'll be rewarded if you help me with this task, but I just don't know if you're up to it.

t_ch: 先说报酬再说事。
t_en: Let's talk about rewards first.

t_ch: 诶不对不对！应该先说事儿，再谈报酬吧？你这人有没有点科研精神啊。
t_en: Oh no no no! Shouldn't we talk about the task first, and rewards later? Do you even have any scientific integrity?

t_ch: 报酬到位啥操作都会。
t_en: With the right rewards, no mission is impossible.

t_ch: 口气不小！不过你可别小瞧了世玲的委托了。
t_en: You talk big, but don't underestimate the difficulty of my assignment.

t_ch: 我们空间站的绩效考核，需要每季度汇总课题实验报告。这段时间你也知道…发生了很多很多事，世玲手头上的课题报告…这个，有那么一丢丢紧张……
t_en: On the space station, performance review requires a quarterly summary report of research projects. As you know, a lot of stuff has happened recently. And I'm a bit nervous about my research report.

t_ch: 所以，特聘助手{NICKNAME}！协助我在这段时间内高效稳妥地完成多项课题实验并整理成报告就是你这次的工作啦！
t_en: So, Special Assistant {NICKNAME}, your mission is to assist me in completing multiple projects efficiently and safely during this time and compiling them into reports!

t_ch: 放心，你只需要为我搜集实验样本和实验用品。其他的工作就交给世玲就好了！
t_en: Don't worry, you just need to collect experimental samples and supplies for me. Just leave the rest of the research work to me!

t_ch: 咳咳，场面话就到这里吧，我们直接进入正题。
t_en: Ahem, that being said, let's get down to brass tacks.

t_ch: 第一轮的实验，主要围绕「反物质军团」中的「虚卒•践踏者」进行。课题就叫作…我想想…就叫作《浅析践踏者四足是否受力均匀》。
t_en: The first round of experiments mainly revolves around the "Voidranger: Tramplers" of the "Antimatter Legion." The research title shall be named... Let me think... It shall be named "A Brief Analysis of Force Distribution on Quadruped Tramplers."

t_ch: 除开一些和践踏者本身有关的样本素材，你还需要去搜集一些<color=#dbc291ff>金属</color>。数量我都整理成备忘录给你啦。
t_en: In addition to getting some samples on Tramplers, you'll also need to collect some <color=#dbc291ff>Metal</color>. The exact amount has been specified in this memo I wrote for you.

t_ch: 这样就搞定啦！来，报酬拿好，一分不少！什么？你问这<color=#dbc291ff>「你好剂」</color>是个啥？
t_en: And that should be it! Come, here are your rewards! What? You don't know what this <color=#dbc291ff>"All Good Potion"</color> does?

t_ch: 这个…你自己看着点用！虽然科员之间都默认这个液剂没有危害，但是具体有什么效果，世玲可没法给你保证。
t_en: It's... you can try it out for yourself! The researchers all agree that this liquid is harmless. But, still, don't come blame me if anything goes wrong.

t_ch: 之后还有多少研究？
t_en: How much research is there afterward?

t_ch: 喏，这个是我的报告笔记，不仅有今天的报告文本，还有之后的一些…研究规划吧。
t_en: Here! These are my report notes — some are today's reports and some are... future research plans.

t_ch: 稍微有点粗略凌乱，你将就着看，看不懂的也别问我，我恐怕也还没想明白呢！
t_en: They're all a bit rough and messy, so just go with it. Don't even bother asking me if you can't understand, since I myself haven't figured them all out yet!

t_ch: 等搜集齐了再给我吧。世玲现在手里还有别的事情要忙呢……
t_en: Just hand the stuff to me once you're done collecting. I have other matters to attend to right now...

t_ch: 实验助手的工作…
t_en: Lab assistant work...

t_ch: 开工开工！上一次你的助手工作完成得很好～这一次也要加油哦！
t_en: Time to get to work! Your assistance was great the last time~ Hope you can keep it up for this mission as well!

t_ch: 第二轮的实验还是围绕「虚卒•践踏者」进行的，不过侧重点不再是它的四足，而是它的「尾巴」。这次的课题就叫做《浅析践踏者的尾巴是否防水》！
t_en: The second round of experiments still revolves around "Voidranger: Tramplers." However, the focus is no longer on its legs, but on its "tail." This time around, the research topic shall be "A Brief Analysis of the Hydrophobic Nature of Tramplers' Rear Appendage"!

t_ch: 除开一些和践踏者本身有关的样本素材，你还需要去搜集一些<color=#dbc291ff>固态净水</color>。数量我都整理成备忘录给你啦。
t_en: In addition to getting some samples on Tramplers, you'll also need to collect some <color=#dbc291ff>Solid Water</color>. The exact amount has been specified in this memo I wrote for you.

t_ch: 搞——定！这份报告非常完美！等我提交给小组长之后就来准备下一场实验哦！
t_en: And thaaaat's that! This report is perfect! After I submit it to the team leader, I will prepare for the next experiment!

t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿，上次的践踏者尾巴报告被小组长表扬了…说世玲的切入点非常刁钻！
t_en: Hehehe, the team leader commended me for that Trampler Tail report, and said that my analysis was very crafty!

t_ch: 看来是时候对新的研究对象出手了！就选那个…那个那个「外宇宙之炎」吧！至于研究内容…嗯！就决定是你了《浅析外宇宙之炎的温度》！
t_en: So, it seems to be a good time for a new subject of research! How about that... that... "Blaze Out of Space"! As for the research title... Well, I suppose it should be "A Brief Analysis of the Thermal Properties of Extraterrestrial Blaze"!

t_ch: 的确非常刁钻…
t_en: That's quite a crafty subject...

t_ch: 这么随便没问题吗…
t_en: Is it okay to be this casual about research...

t_ch: 哎哟，世玲可是空间站的正规科员！「科研」！「科研」的事怎么能随随便便说随便呢？！
t_en: Hey! I'm a bona fide researcher of the space station! It's science! How can you just casually call it "casual"!?

t_ch: 你就尽管去搜集素材就好，别的事不用操心！这次记得多带点<color=#dbc291ff>冰之芯</color>回来哦。
t_en: You just go along and collect materials. Leave the rest to me! Just remember to bring back more <color=#dbc291ff>Core of Ice</color> this time.

t_ch: 世玲的实验工作非常顺利～基本已经过半啦！尤其是上次针对外宇宙之炎的报告，小组长说世玲的结论那是相当的大胆！
t_en: My experimental work is going very smoothly~ Already more than halfway through! For that last report on Blaze Out of Space, the team leader said my conclusion was quite bold!

t_ch: 不枉世玲提前打听，小组长平时最喜欢喝的就是咖啡了……
t_en: Good thing I found out the team leader's fave drink is coffee beforehand... Hehehe...

t_ch: 现在看来绩效考核问题已经不大了，剩下的就是为了满足数量。我们干脆将计就计，跟着上回的「外宇宙之炎」，研究它在金属冶炼方面的应用吧！
t_en: Anyway, now it seems the performance review matter is no longer a big issue. All that's left is to hit the quota. We'll beat them at their own game and use the previous "Blaze Out of Space" to study its application in metal smelting!

t_ch: 这次记得多带点<color=#dbc291ff>金属</color>回来哦，这是实验必备的！
t_en: Remember to bring more <color=#dbc291ff>Metal</color> back this time, which is essential for this experiment!

t_ch: 哎，世玲在想，要不要做一些更前沿的课题实验，开拓一些未知领域呢？
t_en: Hey, I was thinking, should I do some more "avant-garde" projects and venture into the lesser-known areas of study?

t_ch: 目前已经很前沿了…
t_en: You are pretty "avant-garde" already...

t_ch: 诶？嘿嘿，确实小组长也有这么夸世玲的啦！
t_en: Oh really? Haha, I guess you're right. The team leader did say something similar when he praised me.

t_ch: 重新定义课题实验。
t_en: You wanna redefine the whole concept of research itself?

t_ch: 对对对，小组长也是这么评价世玲之前的实验报告的！
t_en: Yep yep, You're right! The team leader did say something similar when he praised me for my last research report!

t_ch: 但归根结底，世玲不能满足于眼下的成绩，我需要更多的独创性！为此，我查阅了诸多资料…发现了一个只有在你的协助下，才能完成的实验！
t_en: But at the end of the day, I can't be satisfied with the current results, I need more innovation! For this reason, I consulted a lot of resources... and found an experiment that can only be completed with your assistance!

t_ch: 实验样本来源于一个胖乎乎的机器人，世玲没有见过，不过拜托你的话，拿到一些样本材料应该没有问题吧？
t_en: The experimental sample comes from a chubby robot. I've never seen it before, but acquiring some sample materials shouldn't be an issue, right?

t_ch: 除开这个机器人相关的素材，世玲还需要一些<color=#dbc291ff>陨铁</color>，交给你啦！
t_en: In addition to the materials related to this robot, I also need some <color=#dbc291ff>Meteoric Alloy</color>. I'll leave this to you!

t_ch: 我就是这个「机器人」大课题的持有者，温世玲！啊不对…这样说是不是有点太不谦虚了？
t_en: I, Wen Shiling, am the owner of this robot project! Oh no... That sounds a bit cheeky, doesn't it?

t_ch: 白日梦做完记得做实验。
t_en: Remember to do the experiments once you finish daydreaming.

t_ch: 还没下班就开始说梦话？
t_en: Already sleep-talking even before getting off work?

t_ch: 我…我没做白日梦！我刚才那是…学台词呢！
t_en: I—I'm not daydreaming! I was just... practicing lines!

t_ch: 上次的实验报告，小组长看了直接没说出话来！其他组员看了，也都敬佩得浑身发抖，大热天的手脚冰凉浑身冷汗。
t_en: The team leader was at a loss for words after reading my last experiment report! And the other team members were also blown away and couldn't stop shaking in admiration. They were so impressed that they were sweating bullets.

t_ch: 最后小组长说，我这次的报告交上去之后，就帮我申请科室独立实验资金。你瞧瞧，已经提前给我放权了！
t_en: In the end, the team leader said that after I finish this round's report, they're going to help me with funding for my independent research. You see, they're starting to move me up now!

t_ch: 所以这次，你除了给我再拿点那个机器人的素材，你还需要带些<color=#dbc291ff>基本食材</color>。毕竟做研究是很消耗能量的嘛……
t_en: So this time, in addition to bringing me some materials for that robot, you also need to bring some <color=#dbc291ff>Basic Ingredients</color>. After all, doing research can take up a lot of energy...

t_ch: 实验助手的工作…
t_en: Lab assistant work...

t_ch: 哼哼，其实世玲不用做完最后一个实验，也能通过绩效考核。毕竟机器人这个课题，已经让小组长对我刮目相看了！
t_en: Well, actually, I can pass the performance review without finishing this last experiment. After all, my robot project has already immensely impressed the team leader!

t_ch: 作为资金申请的基础课题报告已经提交上去了，最后这个…就是纯凑数了！毕竟咱们科研的关键就是个严谨，七份报告，一份都不能少。
t_en: The project report for the funding application has already been submitted. This last one... well, it's just to fill the quota! After all, the key to scientific research is rigor, so there must be no less than seven reports.

t_ch: 不过严谨那都是基础，最关键的还得是创新！从此科研领域，就要留下世玲的大名了！
t_en: But rigor is just the foundation. The most important thing is innovation! Henceforth, the name "Shiling" will be forever etched into the field of science!

t_ch: 至少数量上是很严谨的。
t_en: It's pretty rigorous indeed, at least by the numbers.

t_ch: 你在做一种很新的东西。
t_en: You're doing some new stuff alright.

t_ch: 哼，世玲不是那种过河拆桥的人。你的名字也被我写在实验助手那一栏了，等着站长给你发奖励吧。
t_en: Hey, I am not the kind of person who forgets those who have helped me. I also noted you down as my experimental assistant, so you can wait for the Lead Researcher to give you your reward.

t_ch: 总之这次，你就随意发挥吧。之前做过研究的三个课题样本，你随便选一个就行。
t_en: Anyway, this time, you can do as you please. Choose any one of the three research topics that have been studied before.

t_ch: 除了样本材料之外…再带点<color=#dbc291ff>蛋白米</color>回来吧！哼哼…最后的课题报告，就围绕蛋白米与神秘生物之间的联系展开！
t_en: In addition to the sample material... bring back some <color=#dbc291ff>Protein Rice</color>! Hmph... The final project report revolves around the connection between Protein Rice and mysterious creatures!

t_ch: 实验助手的工作…
t_en: Lab assistant work...

t_ch: 报告，点击，提交！嘿嘿～之后就会收到绩效考核汇报邮件了。嗯…不知道是升职还是升职还是升职呢？世玲好期待哦！
t_en: Report, submitted! Hehehe~ Now I'll just wait for the performance review result. Hmm, will I get a big promotion or an even bigger promotion? I'm so excited!

t_ch: 话说回来，你作为助手还是很靠谱的嘛。怎么样？有没有兴趣跟着世玲以后搞科研呀？我看你很有天赋嘛。
t_en: By the way, you turned out to be a pretty reliable assistant. So, are you interested in working for me in the field of science? You seem pretty gifted at this.

t_ch: 这哪儿比得过你…
t_en: I'm not as gifted as you are.

t_ch: 哎，不必客气，你这块璞玉，世玲稍加指点，以后也能在科研圈发光发热！
t_en: No need to be modest. With my brilliant guidance, even someone as plain as you can have a bright future as a researcher.

t_ch: 搞科研还是不容易。
t_en: Research is too hard for me.

t_ch: 哎，你是懂科研的。干咱们这行，哪儿有容易这个说法呢？
t_en: You sound like you understand the struggle. No such thing as easy in scientific research.

t_ch: 我也不急着你现在做决定，先把这个拿着吧——上面可是记录着「你好剂」的制作配方，世玲亲手整理的。
t_en: But no need to make a decision right away. I can wait. For now, just take this. It's the recipe for the "All Good Potion," which I formulated myself.

t_ch: 正所谓「投资艺术家的最佳时机，就是在艺术家还没出名的时候」。等世玲之后名气大了，这份配方可就有说法了！
t_en: You know the saying, "Get in on an artist early before they blow up." My recipe is gonna be worth a lot when I become famous in the future.

t_ch: 但是你可别拿去商用啊！我还没来得及上交认证呢。我私下做了几次试验，产出都挺稳定的，你自己拿着用应该没问题。
t_en: But this is not ready for commercial use. I haven't gotten around to applying for safety verification yet. I did a few experiments in private which produced consistent results. You should be fine using it on yourself.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，不知道独立实验的资金什么时候发放呢。
t_en: Hehehe, I wonder when the funding for my independent research is gonna come through.

t_ch: 喏，作为实验助手，我也给你备份了一下这段时间我写的实验报告。不可商用，但作为其他研究用途是没问题的。
t_en: Look, I've put together some copies of the research I've done recently. You can have these, but they're for research purposes only, not for commercial use.

t_ch: 世玲可不算亏待你吧！要是换成别人做助手，我可不会给他这么好的「大福利」！
t_en: That's pretty sweet, right? If it weren't for you, I wouldn't have gone through the trouble of preparing such a big bonus for an assistant.

t_ch: 还能用作其他研究？
t_en: They can be used for other research?

t_ch: 那是自然，这些报告可都是很珍贵的，其他人可没有世玲这样的天赋！你不信的话就看看！
t_en: Yes, of course. These reports are all very valuable. No other researcher is as talented as I am. Just take a look, and you will see.

t_ch: 你的好意我心领了…
t_en: Thank you, but I'm good.

t_ch: 哎，别跟世玲客气，给你就拿着！拿着了也就再看看吧，说不定能发现一些新思路，留给我下次使用。
t_en: Come on, don't be shy. Just take these already. Read them through, and maybe you'll come up with some fresh ideas that you can pass on to me later.

t_ch: 我手头上还有别的活儿没忙完呢！这件事的优先级可以稍微延一下，你等我通知再来吧。
t_en: I still have work I need to get done! This can go on the backlog. Come back when I tell you to.

t_ch: 聊聊感应仪的事儿…
t_en: Talk about the sensor...

t_ch: 想不到你还这么关心我的宝贝感应仪！
t_en: You care so much about my precious sensor! That's unexpected!

t_ch: 这次的研究如果顺利发表，我打算再给它做点宣传包装，看看能不能推广成什么…「年轻人的第一件科学探索仪器」之类的。
t_en: If this study can be published without complications, I plan to give it some publicity, see if it can be promoted as "Young people's first scientific exploration sensor" or something.

t_ch: 你可以看看我的研究笔记，说不定你能帮我找到一些别的包装角度呢？
t_en: Have a look at my research notes, maybe you can find another angle to sell this?

t_ch: 你之前说的信息我仔细思考了一下，感觉应该和「大宇宙时间隧道」有点关系。
t_en: I have carefully considered the information you mentioned earlier, and I feel this has something to do with the big universe time tunnel.

t_ch: 你在现场还有没有发现别的什么东西？给我看看，说不定可以推进研究！
t_en: Did you find anything else at the scene? Show it to me. Maybe it can move the research forward!

t_ch: 啊！这是我的研究手稿！之前丢了一部分，我还以为找不回来了！
t_en: Ah! Those are my research notes! I've lost some of them before and thought I'd never get them back!

t_ch: 不过…怎么是碎片？难道说是那个什么「神奇的扑满」偷走了我的手稿，甚至还撕碎了它！
t_en: But... why are they in pieces? Did that Mystery Trotter steal my notes and rip them apart?

t_ch: 天哪，要不是我有誊抄笔记手稿的习惯，那可就损失惨重了！
t_en: My goodness! I'd be in serious trouble if I didn't have the habit of copying my notes!

t_ch: 不过它们是什么时候偷走的呢…难道潜伏在我的身边？难道就是你！
t_en: But when did they manage to steal it...? Could they be hiding next to me? Are you the thief!?

t_ch: 算了算了！疑神疑鬼可不是科学的态度。你没事也多看看我的研究笔记，说不定能给我提供点新思路！
t_en: Never mind. Being suspicious isn't the right attitude for science. You should read my notes more when you have the time. Maybe you can give me some new directions, too!

t_ch: 听说你需要帮助…
t_en: I hear you need help...

t_ch: 啊？哦…哦！确有此事！没想到来的人会是你。
t_en: Huh? Oh... oh! Indeed! I didn't expect you'd be the one to show up.

t_ch: 算了，本来我这个发现也不能让内行的家伙知道…你这外行，倒是正好！
t_en: Ah well, I don't think my discovery can be told to people on the inside, and you, an outsider, are perfect for it!

t_ch: 不过，你必须向我保证，绝对不会将这事儿告诉别人，甚至不能让人知道委托你的人是我。
t_en: However, you must promise me that you will never reveal anything to others, not even the fact that I was the one who asked for your help.

t_ch: 好的，我发誓…
t_en: Alright, I swear...

t_ch: 不错，看上去你已经有为科学献身的觉悟了。
t_en: Not bad, looks like you already have the resolve to sacrifice for science.

t_ch: 保密可以，得加钱。
t_en: I can zip it, but that's gonna cost you extra.

t_ch: 哎，现在的人怎么都这么势利？收费的事情回头细说。
t_en: What? Why are people nowadays so pragmatic? We'll talk about the fees later.

t_ch: 好了，我这里也赶时间。简单来说：我的「第<unbreak>223</unbreak>号自制感应仪」最近有了反应。
t_en: Fine, I'm rushing anyway. Simply put, my Homemade Sensor No. <unbreak>223</unbreak> has begun to react recently.

t_ch: 但…这东西做出来太久了，我也没办法确定它是用来追踪哪种波长的…所以需要有个人帮我去考察验证下。
t_en: But... this thing has been in use for so long, I can't determine what wavelength it is detecting... So I need someone to help me ascertain.

t_ch: 总之，我会给根据感应仪指向的方位，给你一个大概的坐标。而你需要去那里帮我看看，有没有什么异常情况。
t_en: To make long story short, I will give you a direction based on the general location the sensor is pinging. And you just need to go to the spot and look around for anything abnormal.

t_ch: 要是没有异常呢？
t_en: What if there's nothing abnormal?

t_ch: 那肯定是你观察得不够仔细！难不成还会是我的感应仪有问题？
t_en: That's because you're not detailed enough! Or are you trying to say there's something wrong with my sensor?

t_ch: 什么样才算异常？
t_en: What is "abnormal"?

t_ch: 和平常不一样就是异常啊！哎，你到时候去看了应该就知道了。
t_en: "Abnormal" is something that's out of the norm! Gah, you'll know when you get there.

t_ch: 科学精神的可贵之处就在于探索与发现，我就是怀揣着这样的信念才能研究「大宇宙时间隧道」…
t_en: The value of the scientific spirit lies in exploration and discovery. It is with this belief that I can study the big universe time tunnel theory...

t_ch: 诶！诶有反应了！感应仪有反应了！
t_en: Hey, it's a ping! The sensor picked up something!

t_ch: 这「感应仪」看着不太结实…
t_en: The sensor doesn't seem very sturdy...

t_ch: 结不结实不重要！我小心点用就没问题！重要的是：你看到这个反应了没！
t_en: Its sturdiness doesn't matter as long as I'm careful while operating it! The more important thing is: Do you see the object giving out the reading?

t_ch: 哦！有反应了！真有反应了！
t_en: Oh, a reading! There's a reading!

t_ch: 看吧看吧！我没骗你吧！哈哈！这就是我雷金的科学时代！
t_en: See! What did I tell ya? Haha! The Scientific Age of Regin is upon us!

t_ch: 我整理了一下过去的随笔记录，大概还原了「<unbreak>223</unbreak>号」的初始情况。可惜具体的设计用途我不太记得…只知道这个仪器会给我反馈一些「异常」坐标。
t_en: I tidied up the notes I had and have roughly reset No. <unbreak>223</unbreak> to its default stage. Pity I don't quite remember the exact use for it... All I know is, this machine will give me some coordinates of "anomalies."

t_ch: 所以我需要找一位身手矫健，同时对科学也有一定了解的人替我去坐标所在地考察一下是否真的有「异常」。
t_en: So, I'm looking for someone athletic and who also has a good scientific background. They need to go to these coordinates and find out if there are actually any "anomalies."

t_ch: 你可以先看一下我整理的「研究笔记」了解了解。现在「<unbreak>223</unbreak>号」还暂时没有反应……
t_en: You can look at my research notes first. No. <unbreak>223</unbreak> hasn't responded yet...

t_ch: 诶！诶！你瞧瞧，这不就有「反应」了！赶紧赶紧，你来帮把手，我来记录一下坐标！
t_en: Oh! Oh! Look, it's responding now! Hurry and give me a hand! I need to note down this coordinate!

t_ch: 哎！灵感突然进入了我的大脑…这个坐标，感觉像是某种和<color=#dbc291ff>「交易」</color>有关的「物体」…我不好说，你就当是我的小小建议吧！
t_en: Oh! I feel an intuition coming into my brain... This coordinate feels like it's something to do with <color=#dbc291ff>transactions</color>... I can't tell what it is. Count it as a tip from me!

t_ch: 这台售货机并无明显的异常，但眼见不一定为实……
t_en: The vending machine doesn't seem to have any obvious anomaly, but don't judge a book by its cover...

t_ch: （摸摸它）
t_en: (Touch it)

t_ch: 虽然看上去它十分冰冷，但摸上去却似乎有一些温度？你不确定这是否是错觉。
t_en: It looks like it was supposed to be cold, but it felt... warm? You're not sure if your senses are lying to you.

t_ch: （拍拍它）
t_en: (Pat it)

t_ch: 沉闷的回音是唯一的回应，但似乎隐约听见了一些窸窸窣窣的杂音。你不确定这是否是错觉。
t_en: The only response you get is a deep, hollow echo. You do vaguely hear some rustling sounds. You're not sure if this is your imagination.

t_ch: 似乎并没有办法确认这台故障的售货机是否是「感应仪」指向的异常情况。但有一个绝妙的办法可以得到答案。
t_en: There doesn't seem to be a way to confirm whether this malfunctioning vending machine is abnormal as picked up by the sensor. But there is a brilliant way to get an answer.

t_ch: （用拳头殴打它）
t_en: (Punch it)

t_ch: （用脚底猛踹它）
t_en: (Kick it)

t_ch: 好像是错觉，你感受到眼前的售货机似乎正在…瑟瑟发抖？
t_en: You may be hallucinating, is the vending machine... shivering?

t_ch: （雷金所说的异常情况，应该就是指这个了…吧？）
t_en: (This should be the anomaly that Regin was talking about... right?)

t_ch: （所以扑满原来还有这种奇怪的能力设定吗？！）
t_en: (Trotters have strange abilities like this!?)

t_ch: 售货机并没有吐出任何的货物，但却显示交易已完成。
t_en: The vending machine didn't spit out any merchandise but shows the transaction as complete.

t_ch: 这样的「特殊情况」，只能采取一些「特殊措施」了……
t_en: These "special situations" call for "special measures"...

t_ch: （踢踹售货机）
t_en: (Kick the vending machine)

t_ch: 拿出魄力就可以解决问题！尤其是这样的售货机，卡住了就可以帮它「踢」出来嘛！
t_en: Use your charisma to solve the problem! Vending machines like this can be kicked if they're stuck!

t_ch: （猛摇售货机）
t_en: (Shake the vending machine)

t_ch: 拿出魄力就可以解决问题！尤其是这样的售货机，卡住了就可以帮它「摇」出来嘛！
t_en: Use your charisma to solve the problem! Vending machines like this can be shaken if they're stuck!

t_ch: （怎么感觉这个售货机正在…微微发抖？这就是雷金之前说的「与交易有关的物体」吗？）
t_en: (Why is this vending machine... shaking? Is this the "thing involved with transactions" Regin talked about?)

t_ch: 瞧见没？这就是科学的力量！坐标已经给你标好了，快去快去，趁着感应还热乎！
t_en: Did you see that? This is the power of science! I've already given you the coordinates, go go go while the track's hot!

t_ch: （接下来，只需要前往雷金标记的坐标附近探索异常情况就好了……）
t_en: (Now, just need to go to the location Regin marked and look out for anomalies...)

t_ch: 旁听
t_en: Listen

t_ch: 诶，这个怎么没有反应啊？
t_en: Hm? Why is this not working?

t_ch: 是不是…坏掉了？我听别人说，可以踢它两脚。
t_en: Did it... break? I hear you should kick broken stuff twice.

t_ch: 可要是被我们踢坏了，不会需要赔偿吧？我不敢……
t_en: But if we broke it, they won't want us to repay them, would they? I can't...

t_ch: 我总觉得怪怪的…昨天我们来这里，有见过这个吗？
t_en: It feels weird, somehow... When we were here yesterday, did you see this thing?

t_ch: 你，你别吓我啊！
t_en: D—Don't scare me!

t_ch: 难道是坏了？
t_en: Is it broken?

t_ch: 要不…踢它两脚？
t_en: What if... we kick it?

t_ch: 我看错了？
t_en: Did I see it wrong?

t_ch: 要不还是走吧……
t_en: We should go...

t_ch: 不会是我看错了吧？这里刚才应该有个售货机的啊……
t_en: Am I mistaken? There should be a vending machine here...

t_ch: 是不是走错了？我们再往前走走看？
t_en: Did we get the road wrong? Do you want to move over there?

t_ch: 不应该，不应该啊！我再看看……
t_en: No, it shouldn't be! I have to see...

t_ch: 哎呀！别看了！快走吧！
t_en: Stop! Don't look for it! Let's go!

t_ch: 感应仪的反应…
t_en: The sensor's reading...

t_ch: 上次的售货机异常我还没有调查清楚，但感应仪就立刻又有了反应。
t_en: I haven't even gotten to the bottom of the last vending machine, and the sensor's already picked up another signal.

t_ch: 尤其是这次的位置…我不是很方便去。你自己也最好多注意安全！
t_en: The location this time... It's not very convenient for me to go myself. Be on the lookout for any danger!

t_ch: 不安全的得加钱！
t_en: Danger? Extra charge!

t_ch: 又加钱？！加加加，前提是你得出意外。
t_en: Again!? Fine, I'll agree to it, but you have to get the job done.

t_ch: 你有什么不方便？
t_en: What's so inconvenient?

t_ch: 我又不像是你这么神通广大，上天入地样样都行！自然也有能力受限的时候……
t_en: I'm not as powerful as you are, I can't do a tenth of the things you can! Naturally, my personal power is limited...

t_ch: 雪堆下面似乎藏着什么东西…你能感受到积雪正在微微颤抖。
t_en: Something seems to be hiding beneath the Snow Drift... You can feel the layer of thick snow trembling lightly.

t_ch: （猛踹雪堆）
t_en: (Kick the Snow Drift)

t_ch: （清扫雪堆）
t_en: (Clear the Snow Drift)

t_ch: 雪堆下的东西跳了出来……
t_en: Something jumped out of the Snow Drift...

t_ch: （躲在雪堆底下这么久，看来是真的不怕冷啊！）
t_en: (It must be cold-proof if it can hide underneath the Snow Drift for so long!)

t_ch: 阿嚏——！
t_en: Achoo!

t_ch: 虽然科学需要献身精神…但你还是要以个人安全为第一考量，量力而行！
t_en: Science requires sacrifice, but you should still prioritize your personal safety and act according to your capabilities!

t_ch: 雪堆下面似乎露出了衣服的一角，这场面似乎有点眼熟。
t_en: A piece of clothing sticks out from underneath the Snow Drift. This feels familiar.

t_ch: （猛踹雪堆）
t_en: (Kick the Snow Drift)

t_ch: 一脚下去，只有蓬松的积雪，并没有踢到什么实际的东西。
t_en: You kick the Snow Drift and only find purchase on soft, fluffy snow. There is nothing solid in there.

t_ch: （清扫雪堆）
t_en: (Clear the Snow Drift)

t_ch: 你将积雪清扫掉，埋藏在下面的东西逐渐露出了真面目。
t_en: You sweep the pile of snow aside and unveiled the item buried in the snow.

t_ch: 一堆杂乱的衣物被埋在雪堆之下，似乎是在伪造某人躲藏在雪地中的假象。
t_en: A mishmash of clothing articles was buried under the Snow Drift, seemingly to create the illusion that someone was hiding in it.

t_ch: 看这个衣服的配色——紫蓝相间，让人忍不住联想一位名叫桑博的「老朋友」。
t_en: The color palette of the clothes — shades of purples and blues — reminds you of an "old friend" named Sampo.

t_ch: 雪堆下面似乎露出了盔甲的边缘一角。
t_en: A piece of armor sticks out from underneath the Snow Drift. This feels familiar.

t_ch: （猛踹雪堆）
t_en: (Kick the Snow Drift)

t_ch: 哎哟！谁踢我！
t_en: Ouch! Who's kicking me?

t_ch: （清扫雪堆）
t_en: (Clear the Snow Drift)

t_ch: 哎哟！谁在捣乱！
t_en: Hey! Who's messing up my spot?

t_ch: 哎哟…哎哟冷死我了。都赖你！我在雪地里潜伏了好久，差一点就抓到他了……
t_en: Oh... oh dear... so cold. It's all your fault! I'd been hiding in the snow for so long and almost caught him...

t_ch: 喂，你这家伙，没事跑这里来做什么？难不成…你是那个家伙的同伙！
t_en: Hey you, what are you doing here? Are you... are you an accomplice of his!?

t_ch: 你…你给我站住！哎，腿冻僵了…阿嚏！阿嚏！
t_en: F—Freeze! Ohh, my leg's frozen numb... Achoo! Achoo!

t_ch: 算了，放你一马！下次再逮着你妨碍我工作，可不会轻易饶过…阿嚏！
t_en: Gah, you're lucky to get away this time! Next time you stand in my way, I won't let you go so eas— Achoo!

t_ch: 旁听
t_en: Listen

t_ch: 感觉积雪比平时要多一些…难道有什么奇怪的家伙藏在雪堆下面？
t_en: Looks like the snow was thicker than usual... Is something strange hiding beneath the Snow Drift?

t_ch: 感应仪的反应…
t_en: The sensor's reading...

t_ch: 这次的地方已经给你标注好了，你多上点心，可别漏了什么关键线索！
t_en: I've marked the location for you this time. Pay attention and don't miss anything!

t_ch: 勘测我可是专业的。
t_en: I'm a professional scout.

t_ch: 专业的就好！毕竟我也是专业的科学家，团队里的其他人也得是专业的才行！
t_en: Professional is good! I'm also a professional scientist, so my colleagues have to be professional as well!

t_ch: 一个线索不会落下。
t_en: I won't miss out anything.

t_ch: 最好最好！可得仔细地、地毯式地勘测！
t_en: That's good! Check every inch of the place!

t_ch: 遇到危险得加钱。
t_en: If it's dangerous, there's extra charge.

t_ch: 哎，知道知道！等出了危险再说也不迟！快去快去！
t_en: Ack, I know that! Tell me about it when you run into danger! Move it!

t_ch: 要是没线索呢？
t_en: What if there are no clues?

t_ch: 那就是你勘测得不够仔细呗！我是不会给你支付酬劳的！
t_en: Then you're not detailed enough! I won't pay you!

t_ch: 不然你和我一起来？
t_en: What if you come with me?

t_ch: 我，我可是有很多工作要忙的！再说，我请你来就是为了省事，我要是都自己去了，那就不用给你支付酬劳了！
t_en: I, I have a lot of things to do! Besides, the whole reason I hired you was to save my effort. If I have to go there myself anyway, I might as well do it on my own and save on the fee!

t_ch: 刚撕下广告画的一个角，眼前的画布似乎开始…扭动了。
t_en: The second you tear a corner of the advertisement, the canvas begins to... twitch.

t_ch: （这次还挺会隐藏的，只不过它肯定没想到变成广告画有可能被撕下来……）
t_en: (It's pretty stealthy, but it probably didn't think it would be torn off as an advertisement poster...)

t_ch: （趁还没有引起更大的骚动，速战速决吧！）
t_en: (Finish it off before it makes a huge ruckus!)

t_ch: 旁听
t_en: Listen

t_ch: 这个宣传海报…我之前怎么没看到过？
t_en: This poster... Was it there before?

t_ch: 肯定是恶作剧呗！那些青春期的臭小鬼，最喜欢搞这种事。
t_en: Must be a prank by some of those teenage delinquents. They love pranks.

t_ch: 可是我记得没人来这里贴过海报啊……
t_en: But I've never seen anyone pasting up poster in this spot...

t_ch: 你又不是一直盯着这里！要搞恶作剧，肯定都是半夜来的啊！
t_en: Your eyes weren't glued to this place, were they? If I wanted to do it, I'd do it in the middle of the night!

t_ch: 那…那我们应该怎么做？
t_en: So... so what should we do?

t_ch: 建议直接撕了。
t_en: Tear it down.

t_ch: 啊？我看画得挺好的…没必要吧？
t_en: Huh? It looks well done... For real?

t_ch: 烧了也能取暖。
t_en: We can burn it for warmth.

t_ch: 啊？这天气也没那么冷…没必要吧？
t_en: Wha? The weather is not that cold... For real?

t_ch: 瞧你畏畏缩缩的样，还不如这个过路人态度坚定！
t_en: Look at you, so hesitant, even a passerby is more firm than you!

t_ch: 依我看，就听这位{F#女士}{M#先生}的！恶作剧这种不正之风，决不能在贝洛伯格盛行！
t_en: I think we should listen to this {F#lady}{M#man}! We cannot tolerate pranks like this in Belobog!

t_ch: 你分不清它是一个酒壶还是一个罗盘——当然，它也有可能是一个怀表。
t_en: You can't tell if it's a hip flask or a compass... it could even be a pocket watch.

t_ch: 一张图案让人放松的画报，但它背景中大面积的殷红色又让人有些神经紧张，可能这就是艺术的魅力。
t_en: A drawing with calming illustration, but the deep red spreading across the canvas in the background is somewhat unnerving. This may be the power of art.

t_ch: （这个广告画似乎有滚轴控制转动，等一等可以看到其他的画面内容。）
t_en: (This advert seems to be controlled by a rolling mechanism. Wait and you'll see different ads.)

t_ch: 旁听
t_en: Listen

t_ch: 这可不是恶作剧那么简单！吓死我了！
t_en: It's not just a simple prank! It scared me half to death!

t_ch: 要相信科学，这肯定是有人在搞鬼，我这就去报告铁卫！
t_en: Believe in the science! Someone must be behind this, I'll go ahead and make a report!

t_ch: 我的宝贝<unbreak>223</unbreak>号感应仪，可是不会有问题的！
t_en: My precious Sensor No. <unbreak>223</unbreak> will never malfunction!

t_ch: 这是新的么……
t_en: Is this new...?

t_ch: 肯定是恶作剧！
t_en: This must be a prank!

t_ch: 吓死我了……
t_en: So scared...

t_ch: 要相信科学！
t_en: Believe in the science!

t_ch: 感应仪的反应…
t_en: The sensor's reading...

t_ch: 这次的地方已经给你标注好了，你多上点心，可别漏了什么关键线索！
t_en: I've marked the location for you this time. Pay attention and don't miss anything!

t_ch: 双开门的衣柜，里面应该可以容纳很多东西。
t_en: A double-doored wardrobe, it should be able to contain many things.

t_ch: 黑暗之中容易暗藏幽灵，而有的时候，自己也可以是幽灵本身。
t_en: In the shadows, spirits hide. Sometimes, you can be the spirit yourself.

t_ch: （打开柜子）
t_en: (Open the wardrobe)

t_ch: 你费劲力气，却没有办法打开柜子。
t_en: You pull with all your strength, but the doors don't budge.

t_ch: （拍打柜子）
t_en: (Thump the wardrobe)

t_ch: 你只是拍打了一下柜子，就感受里面的「那个东西」正在疯狂地颤抖。
t_en: The moment you start thumping it, you can feel "that thing" tremoring wildly from the inside.

t_ch: 可以确信，异常的现象就在眼前。
t_en: You can confirm that the anomaly is in front of you.

t_ch: （变成一个大衣柜躲在这边…它还能有耐性的。）
t_en: (Hiding by turning into a huge wardrobe... It's quite patient.)

t_ch: （这边人流量比较少，应该不会引起什么恐慌吧？）
t_en: (Not a lot of people are around, this wouldn't cause mass hysteria, would it?)

t_ch: （尝试打开柜子）
t_en: (Try to open the wardrobe.)

t_ch: 柜子被锁了起来，需要找到「钥匙」才可以打开这扇门。
t_en: The wardrobe is locked. I need the "key" to open it.

t_ch: （先在附近找找「钥匙」。）
t_en: (Try to find the "key" around here.)

t_ch: 钥匙会放在那里？当然是最顺手的位置！这个道理看来无论在何处都很适用。
t_en: Where would the key be? Obviously, wherever that is the most convenient! That seems to be a universal rule.

t_ch: 这把孤独的钥匙，唯有匹配上对应的锁，才能发挥它的价值……
t_en: This solitary key will only show its true value when matched to the right lock...

t_ch: （拿走钥匙）
t_en: (Take the key)

t_ch: 希望「它」可以打开有意义的谜题，不要让人失望。
t_en: Hopefully, it can unveil a meaningful puzzle, and not be a disappointment.

t_ch: （用钥匙打开柜子）
t_en: (Open the wardrobe with the key)

t_ch: 毋庸置疑！这就是「它」寻觅的价值。作为钥匙，它已然圆满……
t_en: This is without a doubt the value that it seeks! As a key, its purpose is served...

t_ch: 嗯…好像并不顺利。锁芯并没有如预想中那般丝滑地转动，更没有清脆的「咔嚓」声传来。
t_en: Hmm... It doesn't seem to work. The lock is not turning as smoothly as expected, and there are no crisp sounds of clicking.

t_ch: 给予回应的，是熟悉的「颤抖」与特殊的「异响」！
t_en: All I feel is a familiar "shiver" and some "strange sounds"!

t_ch: （这边人流量比较少，应该不会引起什么恐慌，速战速决！）
t_en: (Not many people are around, so no one should see you. Let's wrap it up quickly!)

t_ch: 没有任何画面显示在屏幕上，似乎已经彻底报废了。
t_en: Nothing is showing on the screen, it seems like it's completely busted.

t_ch: 似乎是每个人的家里都会有的长柜，只会在磨损程度上显示出细微的差异。
t_en: Seems like a long cabinet that can be found in any household, with only slight differences in wear and tear.

t_ch: （打开柜子）
t_en: (Open the cabinet)

t_ch: 很幸运，柜子并没有上锁，你轻而易举地打开了它。
t_en: Luckily, the cabinet is unlocked, you can open it pretty easily.

t_ch: 伴随着陈旧的木头挤压声，只有大量的灰尘进入你的视野之中。异常的现象不在这里。
t_en: With the squeaking and creaking sounds of old wooden furniture, a dust cloud emerge in front of you. The anomaly is not here.

t_ch: （拍打柜子）
t_en: (Thump the cabinet)

t_ch: 你轻轻拍打这个柜子，发出的回响侧面印证了它的弱不禁风——幸好没有踹它一脚，否则它会直接变成破碎的木板。
t_en: You thump the cabinet lightly. The echo it gives out proved its frailness. Fortunately, you didn't kick it, or it might have turned into a pile of broken planks instead.

t_ch: 除此之外，并没有更多的声音，也没有更多的回应。异常的现象不在这里。
t_en: Aside from that, no other sounds can be heard, and no other reaction can be perceived. The anomaly is not here.

t_ch: 我的宝贝<unbreak>223</unbreak>号感应仪，可是不会有问题的！
t_en: My precious Sensor No. <unbreak>223</unbreak> will never malfunction!

t_ch: 这里似乎堆放了很多杂物，难道是有东西…躲在杂物里？
t_en: This place looks like it stores a lot of things, could... something be in there?

t_ch: 感应仪的反应…
t_en: The sensor's reading...

t_ch: 这次的地方已经给你标注好了，你多上点心，可别漏了什么关键线索！
t_en: I've marked the location for you this time. Pay attention and don't miss anything!

t_ch: 这个图案你并不陌生，尤其是它发抖的模样，最为熟悉。
t_en: You're not unfamiliar with this illustration, especially when it shivers and tremors.

t_ch: 这个东西胖胖的！像个大面包！
t_en: This thing is so fat! Like a huge, fluffy loaf of bread!

t_ch: 刚才是发生了什么爆炸吗？哎哟！往后退点！
t_en: Did something explode? Agh! Get away!

t_ch: 很显然，这次感应到的异常情况，就在眼前。
t_en: Evidently, the anomaly the sensor picked up is nearby.

t_ch: （是因为变成了电影机，所以自身的形象也发生了改变吗？）
t_en: (Is it because it turned into a projector that its form changed?)

t_ch: （希望刚才那么大的动静，没有吓到那个老太太……）
t_en: (That was a huge ruckus, hopefully that old lady is okay...)

t_ch: 可是…这个怎么玩呀？{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，你帮帮我好不好？
t_en: But how... do we play with this? Can you help me, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}?

t_ch: （打开开关）
t_en: (Activate the switch)

t_ch: 还没等你按下开关键，眼前的电影机突然出现烟雾。随着烟雾散尽，你看到的……
t_en: Before you barely touch the switch, smoke billows out of the projector. When the smoke has dispersed, you see...

t_ch: 正是那个异常现象！
t_en: That anomaly!

t_ch: 哎，这个东西啊，奶奶也不会用……
t_en: Ahh, Grandma doesn't know how to use this either...

t_ch: 奶奶奶奶！你让我试试看嘛！妈妈之前也夸过我很聪明的！

t_ch: 坏了我来赔！

t_ch: 哎哟年轻人！有钱也不能这么瞎折腾啊！

t_en: If you really want to help, then teach my grandson how to use it.

t_ch: （打开开关）
t_en: (Activate the switch)

t_en: With a press of the switch, the image condensed within the mechanical equipment projects onto the wall...

t_ch: 这看上去似乎是附近的某个地方。不太确定，可以再看两眼。
t_en: This seems to be somewhere close by. If you're not sure, look at it a few more times.

t_en: Wow! Is this round thing playing on a slide? I wanna play on a slide!

t_ch: 这孩子，你还没给{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}说谢谢呢。
t_en: Oh, you. You haven't said thank you to the nice {F#miss}{M#mister}.

t_ch: 闹腾腾的小孩子跑走，老太太也跟在后面离开了。
t_en: The energetic child runs away, and the old lady follows him.

t_ch: 现在，你终于迎来了和它的「二人世界」。
t_en: Now, you are finally alone with it.

t_ch: （关掉开关）
t_en: (Turn off the switch)

t_ch: 没了水，鱼儿怎么游？没了投影，看它还怎么藏！
t_en: How can fish swim if there's no water? How can it hide when there are no more projections?

t_ch: （想不到它这次还挺稳得住气。）
t_en: (Didn't think it'd actually stay calm this time.)

t_ch: 这个东西应该是放在博物馆的吧……
t_en: This is supposed to be in the museum, isn't it...

t_ch: 那我可以近距离观察博物馆里的东西了？好耶好耶！
t_en: You mean I can examine a museum collection up close? Yahoo!

t_ch: 哎哟乖乖，这个还是不要乱动比较好哦？博物馆里的东西，可都是不得了的！
t_en: My dear, best to leave it alone, okay? Anything that can be in a museum is not something to play with!

t_ch: 就一下…我就玩一下嘛！
t_en: Just one touch... I'll just touch it once!

t_ch: 玩一下没事的。
t_en: Just one touch should be fine.

t_ch: 你看，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}都这么说了！
t_en: See? Even {F#big sister}{M#big brother} say it's okay!

t_ch: 反正这里不是博物馆…
t_en: This isn't the museum anyway...

t_ch: 就是就是，这里不是博物馆，不需要受博物馆的要求限制！

t_ch: 哎哟，你们年轻人脑子活络懂得多。要玩就随便玩吧！
t_en: Oh, if that's the case, you youngsters are quicker in thinking, and you know more about this. Up to you, I guess!

t_ch: 谢谢你出手相助…不然…不然我想都不敢想啊！
t_en: Thank you for your help... Or I... I can't even think about what would happen otherwise!

t_ch: 哇！那是啥？是怪兽吗！好耶！我见过怪兽了！
t_en: Wooow! What's that? Is it a monster? Hooray! I saw a monster!

t_ch: 造孽啊…造孽啊！

t_ch: 我的宝贝<unbreak>223</unbreak>号感应仪，可是不会有问题的！
t_en: My precious Sensor No. <unbreak>223</unbreak> will never malfunction!

t_ch: 这个东西啊…
t_en: This thing...

t_ch: 好耶好耶！
t_en: Hooray yahoo!

t_ch: 大守护者保佑…
t_en: Supreme Guardian protect us...

t_ch: 哇！那是什么！
t_en: Wow! What's that thing!?

t_ch: 感应仪的反应…
t_en: The sensor's reading...

t_ch: 这次的地方已经给你标注好了，你多上点心，可别漏了什么关键线索！
t_en: I've marked the location for you this time. Pay attention and don't miss anything!

t_ch: 这个帐篷里没有声音，但似乎有什么东西露了出来……
t_en: There is no sound coming from the tent, but something seems to be peeking out...

t_ch: 好像是…鼻孔？而且不是人类的鼻孔。

t_ch: （摸一下）
t_en: (Touch it)

t_ch: （戳一下）

t_ch: （怎么会有扑满躲一半还忘记把鼻子藏起来的啊！）

t_en: That's right, it can't be wrong! My hypotheses are true! This is your remuneration! If the sensor picks up something else in the future, I'll look for you again.

t_ch: 但是邻居奶奶说，最近街上不太平，好像有怪物。
t_en: But granny said it isn't safe on the streets, there might be monsters.

t_ch: 呜呜…我也想看看怪物……

t_ch: （应该不是这里…换个帐篷看看吧！）

t_ch: 我的宝贝<unbreak>223</unbreak>号感应仪，可是不会有问题的！
t_en: My precious Sensor No. <unbreak>223</unbreak> will never malfunction!

t_ch: （这里有不少帐篷，很适合作为藏匿地点，稍微调查一下吧。）

t_ch: 那…那是啥啊！
t_en: Wh—What's that!?

t_ch: 咦？我帐篷呢？
t_en: Huh? Where's my tent?

t_ch: 怪物怪物！
t_en: Monster monster monster!

t_ch: 这还怎么谈生意…

t_ch: 这桩买卖我十拿九稳！
t_en: This deal is mine!

t_ch: 只要骗过那两个笨蛋，做完这单生意，拿走他们的基础资金……
t_en: As long as I fleece those two fools into completing the deal, and then take their base capital...

t_ch: 诶！你是谁？别在外边偷听！

t_ch: （异常情况应该不在这个地方。）

t_ch: 又是吃这个……

t_ch: 呜…不过家里也没有别的东西吃了。

t_ch: （异常情况应该不在这个地方。）

t_en: *unintelligible sounds*

t_ch: 你的代号是…？

t_ch: …你…代号……

t_ch: 咕叽咕叽…唔……呜呜！
t_en: *unintelligible sounds*

t_en: (I wonder what else it's done in Regin's name...)

t_en: How's the scientific community tidying up?

t_en: Don't bring it up! I'm so mad at those old geezers. They said I had already submitted a similar paper and that it was even full of typos. Ugh, I don't wanna have to go through that again!

t_en: I just wrote this paper! How could it have been submitted before? This must've been the decision of the "big universe theory" branch...

t_ch: 没有什么特殊之处的地髓矿石，看上去色泽和质感也并无异常之处。
t_en: An ordinary geomarrow ore. It doesn't look particularly different in color or texture.

t_ch: 但「异常」往往就是会藏身于随处可见的小东西里！只要这么做，一定可以使它显出原形……
t_en: But anomalies always hide in tiny, commonplace things! Just have to do something to make it show its true form...

t_ch: （给「它」一拳）

t_en: A heavy strike lands on the cold, hard surface of the geomarrow ore.

t_ch: 重重的一脚踢踹在坚硬而又冰冷的地髓表面。

t_en: You didn't find the anomaly, but it's not completely fruitless — at least you found out just how hard geomarrow is.

t_ch: （口头发言恐吓「它」）

t_ch: 可怖的威胁话语从你的嘴里倾泻而出。

t_en: You feel like people who are passing by were giving you sneaky looks, but when you try to spot who those people are, they slipped out of sight...

t_ch: 唯有眼前这块无辜的看板，承受着语言暴力，却仍旧屹立不倒。
t_en: As for the board, it endures your verbal abuse, unyielding.

t_ch: （挥舞拳头恐吓「它」）

t_en: You didn't find the anomaly, but it's not completely fruitless — maybe the next urban legend in Belobog is a strange person who's trying to intimidate a display board?
